volumeTitle: Stichus. Trinummus. Truculentus. Tale of a Travelling Bag. Fragments
label: Trinummus (Section 4.4.1109 - Section 5.2.1177b)

translation.label: Scaife English translation
translation.citation: Trinummus: The Three Pieces of Money

sections.label: Section 4.4.1109
sections.original: Illic sum atque hic sum .
sections.translation: I am both there and here in an instant .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1109b
sections.original: Sequere tu hac me intro .—
sections.translation: Do you follow me this way indoors .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1109c
sections.original: Sequor .—
sections.translation: I follow .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1110
sections.original: Hic meo ero amicus solus firmus restitit ,
sections.translation: This man alone has remained a firm friend to my master ; nor has he allowed his mind to swerve from unshaken fidelity , although I believe that he has undergone many troubles , by reason of the property and the children of my master . Still , this person , as I suspect , alone has maintained his fidelity .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1115
sections.original: Hic homóst omnium hominum praécipuos ,
sections.translation: This individual [*] is the very first of all men ; excelling all in pleasures and delights . So truly do the blessings which I desire befal me , that whatever I undertake is brought about , and constantly succeeds : so does one delight succeed other delights .

sections.label: Section 5.1.1120
sections.original: módo me Stasimus Lesbonici servos convenit domi ;
sections.translation: Just now , Stasimus , the servant of Lesbonicus , came to me at home . He told me that his master , Charmides , had arrived here from abroad . Now he must be forthwith waited upon by me , that the father may prove a more sure foundation in that matter on which I have treated with his son . I ’ ll go . But this door , with its creaking , inopportunely causes me delay .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1125
sections.original: Neque fuit neque erit neque esse quemquam hominem in terra arbitror ,
sections.translation: There never was , nor will there be , nor yet do I think that there is a person upon the earth , whose fidelity and constancy towards his friend equals yours . For without you , it would have been that he would have ousted me out of this house .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1128
sections.original: Si quid amicum erga bene feci aut consului fideliter ,
sections.translation: If I have in any way acted well towards my friend , or have faithfully consulted his advantage , I seem not to be deserving of praise , but I think I am free from fault .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1130
sections.original: nam beneficium , homini proprium quod datur , † prosumpserit ,
sections.translation: For a benefit which is conferred on a man for his own , at once is lost to the giver ; what is given only as a loan , the same there is a right to ask back , whenever you please .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1132
sections.original: Est ita ut tu dicis . sed ego hoc nequeo mirari satis ,
sections.translation: ’ Tis so as you say . But I cannot sufficiently-wonder at this , that he has betrothed his sister into a family so influential .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1134
sections.original: Lysiteli quidem Philtonis filio .
sections.translation: Aye ; to Lysiteles , the son of Philto .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1134b
sections.original: Enim me nominat .
sections.translation: Why , he is mentioning my name .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1135
sections.original: Familiam optumam occupavit .
sections.translation: He has got into a most worthy family .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1135b
sections.original: Quid ego cesso hos conloqui ?
sections.translation: Why do I hesitate to address these persons ? But still , I think , I may wait awhile ; for something is going to be said to the purpose about this matter .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1137
sections.original: Vah .
sections.translation: O —

sections.label: Section 5.2.1137b
sections.original: Quid est ?
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1137c
sections.original: Oblitus intus dudum tibi sum dicere :
sections.translation: I forgot just now to tell you of it in-doors . As I was coming hither , a while ago , a certain swindling fellow met me — a very finished sharper . He told me that he was carrying a thousand gold pieces ,

sections.label: Section 5.2.1140
sections.original: meo datu tibi ferre et gnato Lesbonico aibat meo ;
sections.translation: of my giving , to you and my son Lesbonicus ; a fellow , that I know not who he was , nor have I ever seen him anywhere before . But why do you laugh ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1142b
sections.original: Meo adlegatu venit , quasi qui aurum mihi
sections.translation: He came by my directions , as though he was one bringing the gold from you to me , to give as a portion to your daughter ; that your son , when I should give it to her from my own hands , might suppose that it had been brought from you ,

sections.label: Section 5.2.1145
sections.original: neu qui rem ipsam posset intellegere , et thensaurum tuom
sections.translation: and that he might not anyhow be enabled to discover the fact itself — that your treasure was in my possession , and demand it of me [*] , as having belonged to his father , by the public laws .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1147
sections.original: Scite edepol .
sections.translation: Cleverly contrived , i ’ troth .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1147b
sections.original: Megaronides communis hoc meus et tuos
sections.translation: Megaronides , a common well-wisher of yours and mine , planned this .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1148b
sections.original: Quin conlaudo consilium et probo .
sections.translation: Well , I applaud his device , and approve of it .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1149
sections.original: Quid ego ineptus , dum sermonem vereor interrumpere ,
sections.translation: Why , in my foolishness , while I fear to interrupt their discourse ,

sections.label: Section 5.2.1150
sections.original: solus sto nec quod conatus sum agere ago ? hominis conloquar .
sections.translation: am I standing here alone , and am not forwarding the business that I was intending to transact ? I will accost these persons .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1151
sections.original: Quis hic est , qui huc ad nos incedit ?
sections.translation: Who is this person that is coming this way towards us ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1151b
sections.original: Charmidem socerum suom
sections.translation: Lysiteles salutes his father-in-law Charmides .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1152b
sections.original: Di dent tibi , Lysiteles , quae velis .
sections.translation: May the Gods grant you , Lysiteles , whatever you may desire .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1153
sections.original: Non ego sum salute dignus ?
sections.translation: Am I not worthy of a salutation ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1153b
sections.original: Immo salve , Callicles ;
sections.translation: Yes ; health to you , Callicles . It is right that I should give him the preference : the tunic is nearer [*] the skin than the cloak .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1155
sections.original: Deos volo consilia vostra vobis recte vortere .
sections.translation: I trust that the Gods may direct your plans aright .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1156
sections.original: Filiam meam tíbi desponsam esse audio .
sections.translation: I hear that my daughter has been betrothed to you ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1156b
sections.original: Nisi tu nevis .
sections.translation: Unless you are unwilling .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1157
sections.original: Immo haud nolo .
sections.translation: Nay , I am not unwilling .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1157b
sections.original: Sponden ergo tuam gnatam uxorem mihi ?
sections.translation: Do you , then , promise your daughter for my wife ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1158
sections.original: Spondeo , et mille auri Philippum dotis .
sections.translation: I promise a thousand gold Philippean pieces , as well , for a portion .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1158b
sections.original: Dotem nil moror .
sections.translation: I care nothing about a portion .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1159
sections.original: Si illa tibi placet , placenda dos quoque est quam dat tibi .
sections.translation: If she pleases you , the portion which she presents to you must be pleased as well .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1160
sections.original: postremo quod vis non duces , nisi illud quod non vis feres .
sections.translation: In fine , the object which you desire you shall not have , unless you shall take that which you do not desire .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1161
sections.original: Ius hic orat .
sections.translation: He asks but justice .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1161b
sections.original: Impetrabit te advocato atque arbitro .
sections.translation: He shall obtain it , you the advocate and the judge . On these conditions , do you engage that your daughter shall be given to me as my wife ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1163
sections.original: Spondeo .
sections.translation: I do promise her .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1163b
sections.original: Et ego spondeo ídem hoc .
sections.translation: And I promise her likewise .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1163c
sections.original: Oh salvete , adfines mei .
sections.translation: O save you , my connexions by marriage .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1164
sections.original: Atque edepol sunt res , quas propter tibi tamen suscensui .
sections.translation: But , in good sooth , there are some matters on account of which I still am angry with you .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1165
sections.original: Quid ego feci ?
sections.translation: What have I done ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1165b
sections.original: Meum corrumpi quia perpessu'sperpessus es filium .
sections.translation: Because you have allowed my son to become dissolute .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1166
sections.original: Si id mea voluntate factumst , est quod mihi suscenseas .
sections.translation: Had that been done by my consent , there would have been cause for you to blame me . ⋯ But allow me to obtain of you this one thing which I entreat ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1167b
sections.original: Quid id est ?
sections.translation: What is it ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1167c
sections.original: Scies .
sections.translation: You shall know . If he has done anything imprudently , that you will dismiss it all from your mind . Why do you shake your head ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1169b
sections.original: Cruciatur cor mi , et metuo .
sections.translation: My heart is tortured , and I fear —

sections.label: Section 5.2.1169c
sections.original: Quidnam id est ?
sections.translation: What is it now ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1170
sections.original: Quom ille itast ut esse nolo , id crucior ; metuo , si tibi
sections.translation: Because he is such as I would that he was not ,— by that am I tortured . I fear that if I refuse you what you ask of me , you may suppose that I am indifferent towards you . I won ’ t make difficulties , however ; I will do as you wish .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1172b
sections.original: Probus es . eo , ut illum evocem .
sections.translation: You are a worthy man . I am going to call him out . ( He goes to the door of the house of CHARMIDES ) .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1173
sections.original: Miserumst , male promerita , ut merita sunt , si ulcisci non licet .
sections.translation: ’ Tis a shocking thing if one is not allowed to punish bad deserts just as they merit .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1174
sections.original: Aperite hoc , aperite propere et Lesbonicum , si domist ,
sections.translation: Open the door , open quickly , and call Lesbonicus out of doors , if he is at home .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1175
sections.original: foras evocate : ita subitost propere quod eum conventum volo .
sections.translation: The occasion is very sudden , therefore I wish him to come to me with all haste .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1176
sections.original: Quis homo tam tumultuoso sonitu me excivit subito foras ?
sections.translation: What person has been calling me out of doors with so loud a knocking ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1177
sections.original: Benevolens tuos atque amicus .
sections.translation: ’ Tis your well . wisher and friend

sections.label: Section 5.2.1177b
sections.original: Satine salve ? dic mihi .
sections.translation: Is all quite right ?— tell me .
